hypothesis: Matched architecture probe on `lowrank_grouped_soft_codebook_residual` with `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=2`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, and `LR=3.0e-3` fixed. Final reconstruction formula is `x_hat = U(Vx) + Σ_m pi_m c_m + Σ_i alpha_i b_i`, where `U(Vx)` is a static low-rank trunk library, `{c_m}` is a static soft-codebook library, and `{b_i}` is a static residual dictionary; grouping changes only how the residual support is selected, not the exported decoder basis. This is `扩展兼容`: LUTurbo/Lottable can export it as one static trunk lookup plus one static codebook lookup plus one sparse weighted sum over a static residual library, so inference remains a finite weighted sum over fixed vector banks. At EF=2 its encoder selection cost should remain within the `≤ 1.5×h×n` hard budget because it reuses the same feasible lowrank soft-codebook scorer stack and only constrains support selection. The single question is whether the soft-codebook branch remains worthwhile with cheaper group-local residual competition, or whether full global residual selection is still necessary.
next_hypotheses: If `lowrank_grouped_soft_codebook_residual` is clearly worse than the two-stage mainline in both FVU and runtime metrics, discard grouped soft-codebook variants and return to `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` next round.

If it is modestly worse in FVU but materially better in throughput or total runtime, keep it as a lower-complexity compatible reserve branch, but still return to the mainline next round per probe policy.
If it is unexpectedly close to the mainline at lower selector complexity, allow at most one later family-specific follow-up after the required return to the mainline.
notes_to_memory: Compatibility: `扩展兼容`. Export path is static trunk library + static soft-codebook library + static residual dictionary; grouped competition affects only online selection, not decoder exportability.
This round is a strict matched architecture probe: keep `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=2`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, and `LR=3.0e-3` explicitly pinned, and change only `ARCHITECTURE`.

Evaluate `tokens_per_sec`, total duration, and `final_total_tokens` alongside FVU, because LUTurbo cares about total runtime cost rather than FVU alone; a tiny quality change with slower runtime is negative.
self_review: This obeys the single-variable rule by changing only `architecture` and explicitly pinning the current mainline recipe. After `lowrank_gated_soft_codebook_residual` underperformed and `lowrank_soft_codebook_residual` hit repeated policy-detection issues, the highest-value remaining matched probe is `lowrank_grouped_soft_codebook_residual`: it keeps the same compatible formula class, should stay budget-feasible at EF=2, and tests whether the coarse codebook branch still carries value when residual selection is simplified by local competition.
summary: Run one matched architecture probe on `lowrank_grouped_soft_codebook_residual` with the `K=128`, `EF=2`, `signum`, `LR=3.0e-3` mainline recipe fixed. This cleanly tests whether a grouped soft-codebook single-stage architecture is worth any continued attention under LUTurbo-compatible low-EF constraints.
